title: polygala vulgaris var. oxyptera
intro: Polygala vulgaris var. oxyptera, also known as common polygala, is a plant species belonging to the milkwort family and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of common polygala include its ability to reduce fever, soothe coughs, and alleviate symptoms of allergies. Common polygala can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for consumption, as well as being used in topical applications to benefit from its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
